behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff boundary (classifier -> API client payload). A pure unit test of helper logic can miss wiring bugs where labels are dropped/renamed before dispatch. No real OS/network is required, and no model-output quality is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process fake API client that records outbound requests. Invoke classification with a known taxonomy and assert the dispatched payload contains the full, exact taxonomy label set.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is provider-response handling across module boundaries (API adapter -> normalization/validation -> return value). A unit test of normalization alone may miss integration bugs in response extraction. Higher levels are disqualified because behavior text only requires handling an explicit out-of-taxonomy value, not real model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Stub API client to return an invalid label (for example "medical") while taxonomy excludes it. Assert module result is exactly "unknown".
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Agentic because behavior is explicitly about deterministic invalid-value handling, not classification quality.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is deterministic transformation logic inside normalization itself. No cross-module interaction is needed to expose the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization repeatedly with identical raw response fixtures (case/whitespace/punctuation variants if supported by spec) and assert identical final label each time.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on repeated live model decisions. A stub cannot reveal nondeterministic label drift from the real AI provider. This is a single agentic step repeated, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real API classifications for the same input/taxonomy under controlled settings, record provider raw responses and final labels, and assert label consistency when raw provider label is unchanged; flag drift when provider output changes.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Integration because behavior text requires "live API calls" and consistency of real provider outcomes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Real failure is often at enforcement handoff (budgeting logic -> dispatch decision). Need to verify both transformed request and whether dispatch is blocked/allowed. Real network/model output is unnecessary.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a low token budget and use a fake API client. Submit over-budget input; assert policy behavior (truncate or reject) and confirm outbound request/token estimate respects budget before any dispatch.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System because behavior text is explicitly "before dispatch," so in-process wiring is sufficient.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires real provider-reported usage, and execution path includes a real AI API call. Mocks can hide tokenizer/accounting mismatches and completion-token variability that only appear with the live model/provider.
behaviors.test_description: Run classification against the real provider for representative documents; assert provider-reported total tokens per call are <= configured budget, and capture any overage with request/response metadata.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly relies on provider-reported live usage, which stubs cannot faithfully reproduce.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure is retry orchestration between rate-limit response handling, backoff, and re-dispatch. In-process stubs can reproduce 429/RateLimit responses and verify retry behavior. No real model-quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client scripted to return rate-limit error first, then success. Assert module waits per policy (or uses injected clock/backoff scheduler), retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing immediate error.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System because real network conditions are not required to reproduce the retry-state failure mode.
